city_report.transport: Tickets and Leap Card On trains and trams, tickets can be purchased from ticket vending machines at the station or stop prior to boarding. Train tickets are valid for the day of purchase and are needed to get through the entry and exit turnstiles at stations. Tram tickets do not have to be validated prior to boarding, but must be used within 90 minutes of purchase.

hotel.name: Camac Valley Caravan and Camping Park

attraction.name: Guinness Storehouse
attraction.description: Brewery experience telling the story of Ireland's famous stout.

attraction.name: Trinity College
attraction.description: Oldest university in Ireland, home to the Book of Kells.

attraction.name: Temple Bar
attraction.description: Vibrant cultural quarter and nightlife hub.

attraction.name: Dublin Castle
attraction.description: Major government complex and former seat of British rule.

attraction.name: Phoenix Park
attraction.description: One of the largest enclosed recreational spaces in Europe.

attraction.name: St. Patrick's Cathedral
attraction.description: National Cathedral of the Church of Ireland.

attraction.name: Kilmainham Gaol
attraction.description: Former prison now a museum on Irish independence history.

attraction.name: Ha'penny Bridge
attraction.description: Iconic pedestrian bridge over the River Liffey.

attraction.name: Stephen's Green
attraction.description: Victorian public park in the city center.

attraction.name: National Gallery
attraction.description: Museum housing the national collection of Irish and European art.

city_report.description: Porta d'accesso per le multinazionali tech, Dublino bilancia una crescita economica accelerata con il fascino dei suoi distretti storici. La sicurezza e la qualità dell'aria restano priorità centrali nella sua agenda di sviluppo futuro.
city_report.wiki_intro:
'''Dublin''' (Irish phrasebook|Irish: ''Baile Átha Cliath'', "Town of the Hurdled Ford") is the capital city of Republic of Ireland|Ireland. Its vibrancy, nightlife and tourist attractions are world renowned and it's the most popular entry point for international visitors to Ireland.
As a city, it is disproportionately large for the size of the country with a population of over 2 million in the...
